title: Money & Shopping: Bargains, Budgets and Bills
description: refund, receipt, bargain, afford, withdraw, debt, discount, loan — el vocabulario para manejar tu dinero y comprar con confianza en inglés.

items.explanation: El dinero que te devuelven. GET/RECEIVE/ASK FOR a refund. 'I'd like to get a refund for this product.' El verbo: REFUND. 'Can you refund my ticket?' Diferente de EXCHANGE (cambiar por otro producto).

El comprobante de una compra. KEEP the receipt. 'Keep the receipt in case you need to return it.' La 'p' es muda (pronunciación: /rɪˈsiːt/). Diferente de BILL (la cuenta del restaurante) e INVOICE (factura formal).

'I found a real bargain at the market.' Algo barato o con muy buen precio. FIND/GET a bargain. IT'S a bargain! Como verbo: BARGAIN (negociar precio) — 'You can bargain at street markets.' BARGAIN HUNTING = buscar gangas.

AFFORD + sustantivo / AFFORD TO + infinitivo. 'We can't afford to buy a new car.' 'I can't afford the rent.' Tener el dinero suficiente para algo. AFFORDABLE = asequible, de buen precio. 'The hotel is affordable.'
WITHDRAW money FROM the bank/ATM. 'I need to withdraw some cash from the ATM.' WITHDRAWAL = el acto de retirar. También significa retirarse (de una competencia, un proyecto). Pasado: withdrew.
Cuidado con la pronunciación: la 'b' es muda (dɛt, no dɛbt). IN DEBT = endeudado. PAY OFF a debt = pagar/saldar una deuda. GET INTO debt = endeudarse. 'He's working hard to pay off his debts.'

GET/GIVE/OFFER a discount. 'Students get a 10% discount.' AT a discount = con descuento. A DISCOUNT STORE = tienda de descuentos. También como verbo: to discount = hacer un descuento / desestimar una idea.

'She took out a loan to start her business.' TAKE OUT a loan. PAY BACK/REPAY a loan. A BANK LOAN = préstamo bancario. STUDENT LOAN = crédito estudiantil. También como verbo: 'Can you loan me $10?' (americano, informal).

steps.title: Comprando: devoluciones y gangas
steps.instruction: 4 palabras para el shopping cotidiano. 'Receipt' — la 'p' es muda. 'Afford' va siempre en negativo cuando no podés costear algo: 'I can't afford...'

steps.title: Dinero: deudas y préstamos
steps.instruction: 4 palabras de finanzas personales. 'Debt' — la 'b' es muda. 'TAKE OUT a loan' y 'PAY OFF a debt' son las colocaciones estándar.

steps.title: ¿Qué significa?
steps.instruction: Elegí el significado correcto de cada término financiero.

steps.title: Escuchá y elegí
steps.instruction: Prestá atención a las pronunciaciones con letras mudas (receipt, debt).

steps.title: ¿Dónde se usa bien?
steps.instruction: Elegí la oración con la colocación correcta.

options: I can't afford going to the gym every day.
We can't afford to rent a bigger apartment right now.
She can afford of a new laptop this month.
They afford the vacation in Europe.
exercises.feedback: AFFORD + TO + infinitivo ('can't afford to rent') o AFFORD + noun phrase ('can't afford the rent'). Nunca 'afford of'. La forma más común es 'can/can't afford + noun' o 'can/can't afford to + verb'.

options: I need to withdraw money from the ATM.
She withdrew her money to the bank yesterday.
He withdrew from the ATM to his account.
Can I withdraw money with my credit card deposit?
exercises.feedback: WITHDRAW + money + FROM the ATM/bank. La preposición es FROM (no 'to'). 'I need to withdraw some cash from the ATM' es la oración natural y correcta.

steps.title: ¿Cómo se dice?
steps.instruction: Escribí la palabra financiera en inglés.

steps.title: ¡Lección completa!
steps.instruction: 8 palabras de dinero y compras: refund (reembolso — ask for/get a refund), receipt (recibo — 'p' muda), bargain (ganga), afford TO + verb (poder costear — I can't afford to...), withdraw FROM (retirar — 'b' muda en debt), debt (deuda — 'b' muda, pay off a debt), discount (GET a discount), loan (préstamo — take out a loan, pay back a loan).
